clusters.title: Avoid shared atomic increments on every URMA fast-path request

member_findings.title: Avoid shared atomic increments on every URMA fast-path request
member_findings.impact: Every successful URMA Set/Get now calls RecordUrmaDataPlaneResult and lands in RecordInCurrentWindow, where all client threads contend on the same successCount_/failureCount_ cache lines. On a high-QPS multi-threaded client this adds a cross-core atomic read-modify-write to the RDMA fast path and can reduce the throughput/latency benefit that URMA is supposed to provide even when the data plane is healthy.
member_findings.fix: Replace the single shared counters with a lower-contention design, such as per-thread/per-shard counters that are aggregated only when the window settles, or another batched/sampled accounting scheme that avoids an atomic RMW on every healthy URMA request. Keep the failure-trigger path bounded and preserve the min-sample/window semantics.

member_findings.title: Remove committed AI conversation transcript
member_findings.impact: The new .conversations JSONL file publishes assistant reasoning and tool transcript details into the repository. Even when this instance does not contain usable credentials, these transcripts are not product source and can expose internal implementation notes, command history, environment assumptions, or future sensitive context to every downstream clone and package mirror.
member_findings.fix: Delete the .conversations/8c2f0b5d-3e4a-4b9c-8d2f-1b3c4d5e6f70.jsonl file from the PR and add or enforce an ignore/review rule so AI conversation transcripts are not committed. If any private environment data was present in related local transcripts, rotate or redact it before sharing.

member_findings.title: Remove checked-in assistant conversation transcript
member_findings.impact: This new `.conversations` artifact is not source, tests, documentation, or repo context, yet it records an implementation dialogue and partial intermediate code. Future maintainers now have another large, stale narrative to search through and may mistake abandoned reasoning or snippets for authoritative design context.
member_findings.fix: Delete the `.conversations/8c2f0b5d-3e4a-4b9c-8d2f-1b3c4d5e6f70.jsonl` file from the PR. If any rationale is worth preserving, move a short source-backed summary into the relevant design doc or code comments.

clusters.title: Add negative tests for URMA failover flag validation

member_findings.title: Add negative tests for URMA failover flag validation
member_findings.impact: The PR adds validators that reject non-finite or out-of-range success ratios and zero min-sample counts, but the new UT/ST only exercise valid values. A regression that accidentally accepts NaN, values above 1.0, negative ratios, or min_sample_count=0 would not fail any test, leaving the new operator-facing config surface unguarded.
member_findings.fix: Add focused flag-validation tests, for example in the existing flags/validator test area, that attempt to set urma_failover_success_rate_ratio to -0.1, 1.1, and NaN/inf, and urma_failover_min_sample_count to 0, then assert SetCommandLineOption/validation fails and the previous flag value remains unchanged.

clusters.title: Keep test-only tracker inspection out of the production API

member_findings.title: Keep test-only tracker inspection out of the production API
member_findings.impact: `GetCurrentSampleCountForTest()` is exposed on the production `UrmaSuccessRateTracker` type solely for tests. That makes the runtime class carry test vocabulary and encourages future production call sites to depend on internal counter state instead of the tracker contract.
member_findings.fix: Hide this accessor behind a test-only build guard, move it to a test helper/friend fixture, or expose only production-meaningful diagnostics without the `ForTest` API.
